description:
You are a mobile build engineer doing a quick data-massage job on a CI pipeline host.  
Inside the directory /home/user/build_reports you will find one semicolon-separated log file named build_full_2023-09-15.log whose exact contents are shown below:

ID;Platform;Start;End;Status;Duration(s)
B1021;android;2023-09-15T01:02:03Z;2023-09-15T01:07:10Z;Success;307
B1022;ios;2023-09-15T02:10:00Z;2023-09-15T02:24:45Z;Failed;885
B1023;android;2023-09-15T03:05:30Z;2023-09-15T03:11:01Z;Success;331
B1024;ios;2023-09-15T04:20:10Z;2023-09-15T04:25:35Z;Success;325

Your goal is to create a concise CSV summary that is easier for downstream tooling to ingest.

Required end-state:

1. Create a new file /home/user/build_reports/build_summary_2023-09-15.csv
2. The file must be exactly 5 lines long and must contain only three comma-separated columns in the following order and spelling:

   Build_ID,Platform,Status
   B1021,android,Success
   B1022,ios,Failed
   B1023,android,Success
   B1024,ios,Success

3. Do NOT leave any trailing whitespace, extra blank lines, or additional columns.
4. Solve the task exclusively with classic text-processing utilities cut and paste (a maximum of three to four commands total is sufficient).
5. Do not manually re-type the data; derive all output from the supplied build_full_2023-09-15.log file.

Because automated grading will byte-compare the resulting /home/user/build_reports/build_summary_2023-09-15.csv against the reference shown above, make sure the format matches exactly (including case, commas, newlines, and header text).  
When you are done, you can cat the file to the console so that a human observer sees the final result, but the test harness will rely solely on the file’s on-disk contents.
